source: Stree (2018) — full screenplay; weighted to the 'O Stree, Kal Aana' wall-graffiti device, the male-fear inversion, and the comic-horror tone
love_type: both — sleeper blockbuster that launched a horror-comedy universe, AND cult for its meme-ready 'O Stree Kal Aana' device and feminist subtext
core_emotion: delicious, communal fright-laughter — being scared and amused at once, plus the sly thrill of a horror where the men are the ones cowering

dialogue_style.register: small-town (Chanderi) everyday banter — crude, affectionate, superstition-soaked; humor from ordinary men way out of their depth
dialogue_style.rhythm: comic ensemble cross-talk punctuated by sudden hushed dread (the whispered name in the dark)
dialogue_style.stylization: horror-comedy tonal seesaw — naturalistic banter, heightened scares, then a wink
dialogue_style.why_it's_theirs: the fear vocabulary is gendered and inverted — men protecting men, women in control — which gives even throwaway lines a satirical charge

signature_dialogues.line: O Stree, kal aana (O Lady, come tomorrow)
signature_dialogues.construction: a polite written request to a ghost — defers her by always being 'tomorrow', exploiting her literalism; folk-charm as plot device
signature_dialogues.the_setup: townsfolk paint it on every door to survive the festival nights when Stree hunts men
signature_dialogues.presentation: shown as ubiquitous graffiti, a town-wide ritual the camera keeps returning to — the line lives on walls, not just in mouths, so it's literally written into the world and infinitely screenshot-able
signature_dialogues.payoff: turns a horror premise into a participatory in-joke the whole audience is let in on
signature_dialogues.why_quotable: endlessly remixable template ('O X, kal aana') for memes; printable, paintable, shareable

signature_dialogues.line: Stree raksha karti hai (the Stree protects)
signature_dialogues.construction: twist-reveal reframing — the feared monster is actually a guardian; reversal of the entire premise
signature_dialogues.the_setup: a whole film of dread about a woman who abducts men
signature_dialogues.presentation: delivered as the closing reframe so the audience re-reads everything they feared; a respectful turn that converts a horror gimmick into a statement about a wronged woman
signature_dialogues.payoff: the scare resolves into sympathy and a feminist wink — earns rewatch with new eyes
signature_dialogues.why_quotable: the protective-not-predatory inversion is the discussion-bait that fuels the cult

the_peaks.moment: men nervously painting 'O Stree Kal Aana' on their doors at dusk
the_peaks.why_loved: a town-wide ritual the audience is in on — communal, funny, instantly meme-able

the_peaks.moment: the reveal that Stree protects rather than preys
the_peaks.why_loved: flips fear into sympathy; rewards the audience for staying with a smarter point

wish_fulfillment: a horror world where female power is dominant and male bravado is gently mocked — and a town that survives by cleverness, not strength
character_magnetism: the bumbling small-town male ensemble whose ordinariness is endearing; Rajkummar Rao's hapless everyman charm
audacity: marrying broad lowbrow comedy to a genuine feminist parable about a wronged woman — and making horror funny without defanging it
portability: 'O Stree Kal Aana' as a meme template; the gender-inverted scare as conversation piece
why_cult_not_just_good: it spawned a shared-universe fandom and a reusable meme grammar — fans generate their own content from its template
transferable_love_triggers: Put your catchphrase into the WORLD as text/objects (graffiti on every door), not just dialogue — visible, screenshot-able lines become memes.
Build a participatory in-joke the whole audience is let in on (a survival ritual) — shared secrets create belonging.
Use a final reframe that flips the monster into a victim/guardian, forcing a sympathetic rewatch and fueling discussion.
Invert the genre's default power dynamic (men as the prey) to give even comic lines a satirical charge.
Design a fill-in-the-blank line template ('O ___, kal aana') so fans can remix it themselves.
confidence: high — 'O Stree Kal Aana' and the protective reveal confirmed in script; meme/cult status well documented
